extraction.one_line_summary: VALVERDE won with a late acceleration in final 100m on Mas de la Costa, holding off ROGLIČ after QUINTANA set tempo repeatedly on brutal gradients.
tactical_patterns: QUINTANA accelerated multiple times mid-climb to crack VALVERDE and LÓPEZ but went too deep, leaving him vulnerable in final 300m.
VALVERDE strategically took front at 300m like track sprint rather than launching early, exploiting gradients against premature attacks.
LÓPEZ rode own rhythm when QUINTANA surged, refusing to chase immediately but lost contact when VALVERDE accelerated at 100m.
ROGLIČ sat exclusively on QUINTANA's wheel most of climb, never taking turn, preserving energy for late move but mistimed sprint.

rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: At 39 years old showed tactical maturity by controlling front like track sprint rather than attacking early on 25% gradients.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Positioned perfectly on QUINTANA's wheel entire climb, never pulled, but lacked final acceleration to match VALVERDE despite better placement.
rider_signals.LÓPEZ Miguel Ángel: negative: Cracked when tempo surged despite riding his own rhythm strategy, losing contact at 500m mark suggesting insufficient reserves.
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: Multiple accelerations mid-climb fatigued him for finale despite cracking VALVERDE temporarily, tactical error going too deep too soon.
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: At 20 years old finished in top group with established GC contenders on brutal gradients, revelation performance.
rider_signals.TEUNS Dylan: negative: Lost red jersey unexpectedly, dropped with 5km to go before climb even started after overextending previous days.
